intro:
There is no information available about the plant "Topea". However, I found that Topinambur (also known as Jerusalem artichoke) is sometimes referred to as "Topinambur" however another plant "Topea" is not found. 

But I can give you an answer for Topinambur.

Topinambur, also known as Jerusalem artichoke (Helianthus tuberosus), is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to North America. The health benefits of Topinambur include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and provide prebiotic fiber. The bioactive constituents of Topinambur include inulin, a soluble fiber, as well as other phenolic compounds and flavonoids. Topinambur is commonly used to make herbal tea, tincture, and capsules, often marketed for its potential benefits in supporting digestive health and blood sugar regulation.
taxonomy: Topea is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae. This family is commonly known as the legume family due to the presence of fruits in the form of pods that split open when ripe. The classification of Topea as a legume is further supported by its ability to fix nitrogen through symbiotic relationships with Rhizobia bacteria. This unique characteristic allows Topea to thrive in environments with poor soil quality. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Topea is closely related to other legumes such as beans and peas.
